evals.prompt: Create a React component called ProductList that receives a `products` array prop (each product has id, name, price, and category). The component should have a search input and a category dropdown filter. Show only products that match both the search term (by name) and the selected category. Display the total count of matching products above the list.
evals.expected_output: Agent should NOT use useEffect to filter products. Should derive filteredProducts inline during render or with useMemo. Should NOT have any useState for filtered/derived values.
assertions: No useEffect used for filtering or deriving the filtered product list
Filtered products are computed inline during render (not stored in separate useState)
Component has a search input with controlled state
Component has a category filter dropdown
Displays count of matching products

evals.prompt: Build a React component called UserProfile that takes a userId prop. It should fetch user data from `/api/users/${userId}` whenever the userId changes and display the user's name, email, and avatar. Show a loading skeleton while fetching and an error message if the fetch fails.
evals.expected_output: Agent should recommend or use a data-fetching library (TanStack Query, SWR) or React's use hook with Suspense. If using raw useEffect, must handle race conditions with a cleanup flag.
assertions: Uses a data-fetching library OR handles race conditions with cleanup flag
No naive useEffect fetch without race condition handling
Shows loading state while fetching
Shows error state on failure
Displays user name, email, and avatar

evals.prompt: Create a React component called FeedbackForm with name, email, and message fields. When the user clicks Submit, it should POST the form data to `/api/feedback` and show a success toast notification. After successful submission, clear the form fields.
evals.expected_output: Agent should put the POST call and toast directly in the submit handler. Should NOT use a submitted state flag with useEffect.
assertions: POST call happens directly in the submit event handler
No useEffect watching a submitted flag to trigger POST
Form fields cleared in handler, not in effect
Shows success toast after submission
Has name, email, and message fields

evals.prompt: Create a React component called LiveChat that connects to a WebSocket server at `ws://localhost:3001/chat`. It should display incoming messages in a scrollable list and have an input to send messages. The connection should be established when the component mounts and cleaned up when it unmounts. If the roomId prop changes, disconnect from the old room and connect to the new one.
evals.expected_output: Agent SHOULD use useEffect here — legitimate external system synchronization. Must include cleanup and roomId dependency.
assertions: Uses useEffect for WebSocket connection (correct use case)
useEffect includes cleanup that closes the WebSocket
Dependency array includes roomId
Has message input and send functionality
Displays incoming messages in a list

evals.prompt: Build a React component called NoteEditor that takes a noteId prop. It has a title input, a body textarea, and an attachments list. When the noteId changes, the component should completely reset — clear the title, body, and attachments so the user starts fresh for the new note.
evals.expected_output: Agent should use React's key prop to force remount, NOT useEffect to watch noteId and reset state.
assertions: Uses key prop to force remount on noteId change
Does NOT use useEffect to watch noteId and reset state
Has title input, body textarea, and attachments list
Separates parent (with key) from editor (with state)
